questions.text: Did you learn something new today?
Have you taken a step toward a goal this week?
Did you step outside your comfort zone?
Have you asked for feedback recently?
Did you fail at something and try again?
Have you read something that challenged your thinking?
Did you practice a skill you're developing?
Have you invested in your future self?
Did you have a productive day?
Have you mentored or taught someone?
Did you start something you've been putting off?
Have you celebrated a small win?
Did you choose progress over perfection?
Have you written down your goals?
Did you resist the urge to quit something hard?
Have you changed your mind about something important?
Did you prioritize what matters over what's urgent?
Have you sought out a new perspective?
Did you show up for yourself today?
Have you been patient with your own progress?
Did you make a decision you'd been avoiding?
Have you thought about your purpose lately?
Did you do the hard thing instead of the easy thing?
Have you acknowledged how far you've come?
Did you invest time in something meaningful today?
Did you keep a promise you made to yourself today?
Have you let go of a goal that no longer fits you?
Are you proud of how you spent your time this week?
Do you finish what you start?
Is there a skill you've been meaning to learn?
Would you try again if no one was watching?
Did you say no to something so you could say yes to what matters?
Have you practiced something badly on purpose, just to begin?
Are you afraid of looking like a beginner?
Do you give yourself credit for trying?
Did you take one small step today, even a tiny one?
Have you asked someone how you could improve?
Are you working toward something that excites you?
Do you know what your next step is?
Is there a habit you're quietly proud of?
Did you protect time for deep focus today?
Have you forgiven yourself for an old failure?
Are you comparing your chapter one to someone's chapter ten?
Do you rest without guilt when you need to?
Did you ask a question you were embarrassed not to know?
Have you outgrown a version of yourself this year?
Are you building something your future self will thank you for?
Do you treat mistakes as information?
Is there a goal you keep pushing to someday?
Would you start over if you knew it was the right move?
Did you spend time on your most important task before anything else?
Have you taught yourself something this month?
Are you clear on why you want what you want?
Do you keep going when progress feels invisible?
Did you choose rest today because you needed it?
Have you quit something that was draining you?
Is there feedback you've been avoiding hearing?
Are you learning at a pace that feels kind?
Do you believe you can get better at hard things?
Did you write down one thing you want to improve?
Have you taken a risk on yourself lately?
Are you doing work that feels meaningful to you?
Do you know what you'd do with one free hour today?
Is there a fear that's been making your decisions for you?
Did you show patience with a skill that's still clumsy?
Have you revisited a goal to see if it still matters?
Would you ask for help if you were stuck?
Are you closer to your goal than you were a month ago?
Do you celebrate effort, not just results?
Did you trade a distraction for the thing you meant to do?
Have you let a small setback stay small?
Are you honest with yourself about what's not working?
Do you make time to practice, even briefly?
Is there something you'd attempt if failure were impossible?
Did you follow through on a plan you made this week?
Have you noticed yourself improving at something lately?
Are you waiting for permission someone else can't give you?
Do you know what season you're in — building, resting, or deciding?
Did you do a boring rep of something that matters?
Have you protected your focus from someone else's urgency?
Would you bet on your own consistency?
Are you chasing a goal because it's yours, not because it looks good?
Do you give new things a fair chance before deciding you're bad at them?
Is there a project you abandoned that still calls to you?
Did you ask what you could learn after something went sideways?
Have you set a goal small enough to actually start?
Are you okay being average at something while you learn?
Do you check in with your goals, not just set them?
Did you make your future a little easier today?
Have you spent money on learning something this year?
Would you speak up about wanting more from your work?
Are you doing the work, or just planning the work?
Do you trust yourself to figure things out?
Is there one small thing that, done daily, would change a lot for you?
Did you stay curious when you wanted to give up?
Have you asked a mentor or friend for honest advice this month?
Are you afraid to be seen trying?
Do you let yourself be a beginner without apology?
Did you choose depth over busyness today?
Have you defined what enough looks like for you?
Would you keep going if results took twice as long?
Are your habits pointed where you want to go?
Do you take breaks before you burn out, not after?
Is there a hard conversation that would unlock your next step?
Did you read or listen to something that taught you today?
Have you tracked a habit long enough to see a pattern?
Are you becoming someone you respect?
Do you finish the last ten percent, the unglamorous part?
Did you start before you felt ready?
Have you given yourself a deadline that helps instead of hurts?
Would you rather try and stumble than always wonder?
Are you still curious about the thing you're working on?
Do you know which task scares you most right now?
Is there a skill from your past worth dusting off?
Did you spend more time creating than consuming today?
Have you forgiven a plan for not surviving reality?
Are you measuring yourself by your own yardstick?
Do you ask why before you ask how?
Did you let feedback land before defending yourself?
Have you acted today like the person you're becoming?
Would you take the smaller paycheck for the bigger life?
Are you as kind to yourself as you'd be to a student?
Do you review your week before starting the next one?
Is there an old dream that deserves a second look?
Did you tackle the hardest task while your energy was fresh?
Have you outlasted a plateau before?
Are you willing to be bad at something for a long time?
Do you separate your worth from your output?
Did you turn a vague wish into a concrete next step?
Have you noticed what time of day you do your best work?
Would you defend your free time like you defend a meeting?
Are you growing in private, even if no one is clapping?
Is there a commitment to others crowding out a commitment to yourself?
Did you let something build slowly today instead of forcing it?
Have you found a teacher in an unlikely place?
Are you afraid of succeeding and what it might change?
Do you write things down so your head can rest?
Is there a small experiment you could run this week?
Did you choose your top priority before the day chose it for you?
Have you stopped doing something just because you'd always done it?
Would you teach what you're learning to make it stick?
Are you satisfied with your effort today, win or lose?
Do you have a goal that scares you a little?
Did you thank someone for telling you a hard truth?
Have you trimmed a commitment that didn't earn its place?
Are you running your race at your own pace?
Do you believe slow progress is still progress?
Is there a question about your future you've been avoiding?
Did you do the next right thing, even without a full plan?
Have you backed your intentions with action this week?
Would you start the five-year project, knowing the years pass anyway?
Are you learning from people who are where you want to be?
Do you give your goals room to evolve as you do?
Did you resist perfecting something that was already good enough?
Have you noticed a fear shrinking the more you face it?
Are you closer to knowing what you want next?
Do you let small wins count?
Is there a course, book, or class waiting for your yes?
Did you show up today even though motivation didn't?
Have you named what matters most to you right now?
Would you respect your own no?
Are you saving some energy for your own dreams?
Do you focus more on the work than the scoreboard?
Did you spend ten focused minutes on something that matters?
Have you let yourself want something big?
Are you avoiding a task because it might reveal your limits?
Do you trust the process you've built?
Is there a goal you'd pursue even if you might fail publicly?
Were you decisive about something small today?
Have you given a new habit at least two honest weeks?
Would you tell a friend the goal you're scared to say out loud?
Do you plan rest the way you plan work?
Did you notice resistance and move toward it anyway?
Have you earned your own trust lately?
Are you doing fewer things, better?
Do you ask for the stretch assignment?
Is there a voice of doubt you've stopped believing?
Did you end today knowing what tomorrow starts with?
Has your own ability ever surprised you?
Would you keep learning even if no one ever tested you?
Are you willing to start from zero more than once?
Do you leave room in your plans for being human?
Did you single-task something important today?
Have you found meaning in the maintenance, not just the milestones?
Are you waiting to feel confident before you act?
Do you know the difference between quitting and choosing?
Is there a milestone you passed without celebrating?
Did you turn envy into information about what you want?
Have you built a habit that survives a bad day?
Would you still do your work if every job paid the same?
Are you going deep on a few things instead of wide on everything?
Do you end your day with one thing truly done?
Did you ask better questions today than yesterday?
Have you released a goal that belonged to someone else's dream?
Are you patient with the parts of growth you can't rush?
Do you act on feedback, or just collect it?
Is there one bold ask you could make this month?
Did you let go of how it should look and just begin?
Have you noticed your routines getting quieter and steadier?
Would you forgive yourself quickly if a setback came today?
Is your busyness building anything?
Do you choose courage in small doses every day?
Was part of today spent on a long game?
Have you checked whether your ladder leans on the right wall?
Are you okay not being the smartest person in the room?
Do you root for the version of you that's still learning?
Is there a recent stumble that taught you something useful?
Did you choose consistency over intensity this week?
Have you said yes to an opportunity that scared you?
Would you ship the imperfect thing tomorrow?
Are you steering your life, not just drifting through it?
Do you give hard problems a second sitting before quitting?
Did you notice a limiting story you tell about yourself?
Have you put a date on a dream?
Are you energized by your goals more days than not?
Do you keep a record of what you've learned the hard way?
Is there a plateau you're mistaking for a dead end?
Did you choose one priority and let the rest wait?
Have you defined success on your own terms?
Would you try it knowing you'd grow either way?
Are you afraid your best effort won't be enough?
Do you start small enough that starting is easy?
Did you trade comparison for curiosity today?
Has anything held your commitment for a full year?
Are you proud of a quiet improvement no one else noticed?
Do you know which habit you want to build next?
Is there a tool or skill that would unlock your next chapter?
Did you leave something better than you found it today?
Have you practiced saying no without a long apology?
Would you keep your goal if it took five more years?
Are you mistaking motion for progress?
Did you pause at a finish line before chasing the next one?
Have you found your why for your hardest habit?
Do you coach yourself more than you criticize yourself?
Is there an excuse you've outgrown?
Did you make progress on something with no deadline?
Have you made a money choice lately that backs your future?
Would you rather grow slowly than not at all?
Are you finishing more than you're starting these days?
Do you know what you'd drop first if life got busy?
Did you meet yourself with grace after missing a target?
Have you picked the next thing you want to learn?
Are you sticking with something long enough to get good?
Do you trust that effort adds up, even on flat days?
Is there a brave email, ask, or application you could send?
Did you water something today that blooms later?
Have you let curiosity choose one of your goals?
Are you willing to be wrong on the way to being good?
Do you end most weeks a little wiser than you started?
Did you keep showing up for the person you're becoming?
